key_suppliers: Wyoming Powder River Basin coal suppliers
Southern Star Central Gas Pipeline, Inc.
supply_chain.dependencies: Coal supply from Wyoming Powder River Basin; uranium supply contracts through 2030; natural gas transportation arrangements expiring 2025-2030; single vendor for nuclear fuel assembly fabrication through 2045; single vendor for power island equipment for new natural gas plants
risks: Supply chain disruptions and inflation; tariffs on imported goods; single-source supplier risk for nuclear fuel fabrication; vendor delays for natural gas plant equipment; coal and rail transportation contract renewals; uranium import restrictions from Russia
Fuel price volatility; supply chain lead times and supply constraints due to industry-wide infrastructure investments

regulatory_risks: Wildfire liability and regulatory penalties
GHG emissions regulations and potential carbon tax
Rate moratoriums, rate refunds, limits on rate increases, or rate reductions
Securitization mechanisms eliminating future returns on coal-fired generation investments
Federal Energy Regulatory Commission (FERC) wholesale regulation
Prices established by FERC, KCC, and MPSC may not be sufficient to recover costs or provide adequate return on investment
NERC and SPP transmission system reliability and cybersecurity standards compliance
Nuclear Regulatory Commission (NRC) requirements for Wolf Creek nuclear plant
Greenhouse Gas regulations - EPA final rules on CO2 limitations for new and existing fossil fuel EGUs, including carbon capture and sequestration requirements
PISA annual limits (1.5% Kansas, 2.5% Missouri) may prevent full cost recovery
Water discharge regulations - EPA Effluent Limitation Guidelines (ELG) requiring zero liquid discharge for certain wastewater streams
Regulatory disallowances of prudently incurred costs
NRC licensing and safety requirements for Wolf Creek nuclear facility
Environmental permit renewals with more stringent conditions
Ozone Interstate Transport Federal Implementation Plans (ITFIP) - Potential material impact on operations if finalized for Kansas
Stranded asset risk if coal generation retirements deemed imprudent by regulators
Rate regulation by Kansas Corporation Commission (KCC) and Missouri Public Service Commission (MPSC) - risk of non-approval of full cost recovery or reasonable return on investment
MPSC and KCC operational standards (vegetation management, facilities inspection, quality of service)
Ozone Interstate Transport State Implementation Plans (ITSIP) - EPA disapproval of state plans could require additional emission reductions
IRS interpretation of production tax credits for nuclear and renewable energy
Coal combustion residuals (CCR) Part A and Part B rule compliance
Coal Combustion Residuals (CCR) regulations - EPA expansion to legacy surface impoundments and historic CCR placements
Regulatory lag in rate-setting process causing cost-recovery shortfalls
EPA environmental regulations (Clean Air Act, Clean Water Act, Resource Conservation and Recovery Act)
FERC wholesale rates and transmission regulation
Mercury and Air Toxics Standards (MATS) - EPA tightening emission limits for particulate matter and mercury
Climate disclosure rules issued by SEC increasing compliance costs
EPA environmental justice inspections and increased monitoring
Regional Haze Rule - State or EPA revised SIP or FIP could require operational or physical modifications to generating units
Changes in tax laws, rates, credits, and incentives under new presidential administration
Particulate Matter National Ambient Air Quality Standards - EPA lowering PM2.5 standards could require compliance measures
Property tax regulations and changes in Kansas and Missouri
Compliance with Clean Air Act provisions
Deregulation or restructuring of electric utility industry in Missouri or Kansas

sanction_risks: Prohibiting Russian Uranium Imports Act limiting uranium from Russian Federation
Russian-sourced nuclear fuel contract beginning 2025 impacted by import restrictions
